title: anthyllis depressa
intro: Anthyllis depressa, also known as kidney vetch or alpestris, is a perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe, known for its unique, depressed calyx. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects. It is believed to have various health benefits, including aiding in the treatment of kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and joint pain, as well as promoting digestion and reducing fever. Anthyllis depressa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or dried extract, and is also used in combination with other herbs in various remedies.
